steps.title: Find the common denominator and combine fractions on the right side
steps.content:
The equation is $\frac{1}{R} = \frac{1}{p} + \frac{1}{q} + \frac{1}{s} + \frac{1}{13}$. To add the fractions on the right, the common denominator is $13pqs$.

Convert each fraction:
  • $\frac{1}{p} = \frac{13qs}{13pqs}$
  • $\frac{1}{q} = \frac{13ps}{13pqs}$
  • $\frac{1}{s} = \frac{13pq}{13pqs}$
  • $\frac{1}{13} = \frac{pqs}{13pqs}$

Adding these fractions: $$\frac{1}{R} = \frac{13qs + 13ps + 13pq + pqs}{13pqs}$$

steps.title: Take the reciprocal of both sides to solve for R
steps.content: Since $\frac{1}{R}$ equals a single fraction, taking the reciprocal of both sides isolates $R$: $$R = \frac{13pqs}{13qs + 13ps + 13pq + pqs}$$ That matches choice B — B is correct.
